title: Rhea Seehorn's Golden Globes 2026 Win Made Up For 10 Years Of Snubs
text: Rhea Seehorn won the 2026 Golden Globe for Best Actress in a TV Drama for her role as Carol Sturka in the Apple TV sci-fi series "Pluribus." The award was widely predicted, as her performance was considered exceptional despite competing against strong nominees including Kathy Bates, Britt Lower, Helen Mirren, Bella Ramsey, and Keri Russell. Seehorn's win represents vindication after years of being overlooked by major award bodies. During "Better Call Saul"'s seven-year run (which ended in 2023), she received no Golden Globe nominations for her acclaimed role as Kim Wexler, despite the show itself garnering nominations in the drama category. Her Emmy recognition came late: "The Emmys didn't nominate her for an award until 2022 and 2023, and she didn't win either year." She received Critics' Choice nominations in 2019, 2021, and 2023 without winning, and found limited success at smaller genre-focused awards like the Saturn Awards and Satellite Awards. What makes her "Pluribus" victory particularly meaningful is that other "Better Call Saul" cast members received consistent recognition while Seehorn was repeatedly passed over, making her current Golden Globe win especially satisfying.
